item.title: After ‘Baby Reindeer,’ Richard Gadd Confronts Male Sexual Repression
item.description: The writer and actor found unexpected success by sharing his trauma. Now he’s exploring male pain in a new way.

item.title: I’m the Family Breadwinner. Do I Have to Fund My Wife’s Bad Habit?
item.description: I have never said no to something she has asked for, and this is creating a division between us.

item.title: ‘I Am the News’: The Absurd Drama (and High Stakes) of the Don Lemon Affair
item.description: A Minnesota church protest made the former anchor a star again — and a target for the Trump administration.

item.title: ‘A Mass Disaster Nonstop’: Inside the Turmoil at Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s C.D.C.
item.description: Forty-three current and former C.D.C. employees on the changes they say are replacing science with ideology — and making Americans more vulnerable.

item.title: Saving Hermit Crabs by Breeding Them in the Suburbs
item.description: Often treated as throwaway pets, hermit crabs can live 50 years. Mary Akers, a self-taught expert, wants people to appreciate them as much as she does.

item.title: Can Seniors Order Off the Kids’ Menu?
What Is YouTube’s Dominance Doing to Us? We Asked Its C.E.O.
item.description: Neal Mohan on A.I. slop, parental controls and his platform’s impact on our lives.

item.title: Can We Ask Our Son to Go on Weight-Loss Drugs in Exchange for a House?
item.description: My husband wants to condition the gift on our son’s getting on a GLP-1 program. I feel that a gift is a gift and you should not extort a grown man.

item.title: They Grew Up With Smartphones. This Is How They Live Without Them.
item.description: We asked six young New Yorkers who have taken the leap.

item.title: Is There Life After Smartphones?
item.description: What happens when a growing cohort of young people decide to ditch their devices?

item.title: The Iran War Is Revealing the Messy Middle of Our Renewable Energy Transition
item.description: When the world map of literal power changes, the political hierarchy shifts, too.

item.title: How Russia Weaponized the Cold Ukrainian Winter
item.description: Inside one Kyiv neighborhood as it braved the harshest conditions since World War II.

item.title: What One Month of Intense Red-Light Therapy Did to My Mind
item.description: Celebrities are obsessed. People swear it gives them thicker hair and perfect skin. But what if the biggest effect of red light is stranger than that?

item.title: Why Some Teenage Girls Are Trading Medicine for MAHA
item.description: Disillusioned with doctors, they went on a search for answers. They found supplements and a lot of red meat.
